topic.what: ground-truth-based methodology for evaluating automated vulnerability detection tools
topic.how: constructs curated dataset mapping OSV vulnerabilities to concrete package versions enabling systematic comparison across detection systems
topic.why_matters: heterogeneous vulnerability data sources and inconsistent identifier schemes complicate reliable evaluation of detection tools

characteristics.what_it_measures: number of package ecosystems included in evaluation
characteristics.evidence: Table I lists npm, Maven, NuGet, PyPI advisories
characteristics.context: ecosystems selected for comparative evaluation of detection tools

characteristics.what_it_measures: systematic differences analyzed between vulnerability detection systems
characteristics.evidence: Section VI reports evaluation results across detection systems
characteristics.context: analysis of how different tools interpret vulnerability data

characteristics.what_it_measures: availability of tool to reconstruct dataset from current OSV
characteristics.evidence: authors developed open-source tool for automatic dataset reconstruction

novelty: methodology for constructing ground-truth dataset mapping vulnerabilities to package versions
open-source reconstruction tool for reproducible datasets
systematic analysis of detection system differences
open_problems: dataset represents snapshot at evaluation time not current OSV state
limited number of detection tools evaluated
version range interpretation inconsistencies persist across tools
